title: Manchester City confirms Elliot Anderson signing from Nottingham Forest for £116 million
dek: Manchester City completed the signing of midfielder Elliot Anderson from Nottingham Forest for £116 million on a five-year contract through 2031, according to ESPN. Anderson chose City over Manchester United and underwent his medical at England's training base in Kansas City during World Cup negotiations.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Manchester City signed Elliot Anderson for £116 million on a five-year deal through 2031.
bottom_line: Watch for Anderson's integration into Maresca's midfield system during preseason and the opening fixtures. If Anderson fails to establish consistent playing time or if City's midfield structure undergoes further changes, the strategic value of the £116 million investment will come into sharper focus.
body: Manchester City completed the signing of midfielder Elliot Anderson from Nottingham Forest for £116 million on a five-year contract through 2031, per ESPN's reporting.

The 23-year-old Anderson chose Manchester City over Manchester United, signaling the defending champions' continued pull in the midfield market. At the time the deal was agreed in June, the transfer was a record for a British player, though Morgan Rogers' £117 million move from Aston Villa to Chelsea has since surpassed it, according to ESPN.

Anderson underwent his medical at England's training base in Kansas City, with Manchester City concluding negotiations with Nottingham Forest during the World Cup window. In statements reported by ESPN, Anderson said: "As soon as I knew they wanted me, I was absolutely determined to make this move happen." He added, "City are one of the biggest clubs in the world and the squad they have is amazing and so strong in every position," and "I think they're winners, they're relentless, and that's the sort of team I want to be a part of."

The signing arrives under new manager Enzo Maresca, who has been chosen as Pep Guardiola's heir at Manchester City. The brief does not specify Anderson's exact tactical role or how his arrival affects the status of existing City midfielders, nor does it detail the performance metrics or contract incentives that informed City's confidence in the investment.
